title: Sabalenka Achieves Sunshine Double with Miami Open Victory
summary: Aryna Sabalenka, the world number one from Belarus, defeated Coco Gauff 6-2, 4-6, 6-3 to win the Miami Open and retain her title. This victory marks her completion of the 'Sunshine Double,' as she previously won Indian Wells, making her the fifth woman in history and the first since Iga Swiatek in 2022 to achieve this feat. Sabalenka has had an exceptional start to 2026, winning three WTA Tour titles and 23 of 24 matches, with her only loss in the Australian Open final to Elena Rybakina. In the Miami final, Sabalenka started strong but faced a comeback from Gauff, who saved break points and won the second set. Sabalenka credited her mental strength and preparation for the win, while Gauff will rise to third in the world rankings despite the loss. The match highlighted the competitive rivalry between the two players, with their head-to-head record now at 7-6 in Sabalenka's favor.

background_read: The Miami Open is a prestigious tennis tournament part of the WTA Tour, held annually in Florida. The 'Sunshine Double' refers to winning both the Indian Wells and Miami events in the same season, a rare achievement that demonstrates a player's dominance on hard courts. Aryna Sabalenka and Coco Gauff are top-ranked players with a competitive history, often facing off in major finals. Tennis rankings are updated based on tournament performances, influencing players' seeding and prestige. The sport involves strategic play, physical endurance, and mental toughness, as seen in this match where Sabalenka's resilience secured her victory.
Article_Structure: Main Points: Sabalenka won the Miami Open, completing the Sunshine Double and extending her strong 2026 record. Purpose: To report on Sabalenka's achievement and its historical significance in women's tennis. Evidence Evaluation: The article uses match scores, player statistics, and quotes from Sabalenka, providing reliable data from the tournament. Author Credibility: As a BBC Tennis article, it likely comes from professional sports journalists with expertise in tennis reporting. Methodology: Information is gathered from match results, official WTA data, and post-match interviews, offering a comprehensive view of the event.
